channel.title: utf8 on IT notes
channel.description: Recent content in utf8 on IT notes

item.title: Syslogd 8 bit
item.description: If want to log full utf-8 strings “emoji’s” use the option -8, example on /etc/rc.conf: syslogd_flags="-ssC8" The optiosn are: -s Operate in secure mode. Do not log messages from remote machines. If specified twice, no network socket will be opened at all, which also disables logging to remote machines. -C Create log files that do not exist (permission is set to 0600). -8 Tells syslogd not to interfere with 8-bit data.
